elements.sent-2411-003: The heat engine of magnetic black holes in Einstein-AdS gravity coupled to rational nonlinear electrodynamics, as the working substance, is studied.
elements.sent-2411-004: The dynamical negative cosmological constant is considered as a thermodynamic pressure.
elements.sent-2411-005: We investigate the efficiency of black hole heat engines in extended space thermodynamics for rectangle closed path in the $P - V$ plane and the maximally efficient Carnot cycles.
elements.sent-2411-006: The exact efficiency formula which is written in terms of the mass of the black hole is obtained.
elements.sent-2411-007: It was demonstrated that the black hole efficiency decreases when the nonlinear electrodynamics coupling increases and the black hole efficiency increases if the magnetic charge increases.
elements.sent-2411-008: The relation between the efficiency, event horizon radiuses (entropy) and pressure is obtained.
elements.sent-2411-009: We study an efficiency of the holographic heat engine of a cycle in the vicinity of a critical point.
elements.sent-2411-010: Thus, the heat engine of our model can produce work.
elements.sent-2411-011: Keywords: Gravity; nonlinear electrodynamics; black holes; thermodynamics; heat engine.
elements.sent-2411-012: In extended phase space black hole thermodynamics the negative cosmological constant, which is the dynamical variable, is connected with the pressure, $P=-\Lambda/(8\pi)$ \cite{Kastor,Dolan1,Dolan2,Dolan3,Cvetic2 .}
elements.sent-2411-013: Then the first law of black hole thermodynamics is formulated as \begin{equation}d M = TdS + VdP + \Phi dq, \label{1}\end{equation} where the black hole mass $M$ is treated as an enthalpy, $V$ is the geometric volume $4\pi r^3_+/3$ ($r_+$ is the event horizon radius), $q$ is a charge, and $\Phi$ is a potential.
elements.sent-2411-014: When Einstein's gravity in Anti de Sitter (AdS) space couples to nonlinear electrodynamics (NED), the coupling $\beta$ is the thermodynamic variable conjugated to so called vacuum polarization \cite{Mann .}
elements.sent-2411-015: In this case the first law of black hole thermodynamics (1) is modified by adding the term ${\cal B d\beta$ with ${\cal B}$ being the conjugate to coupling $\beta$.}
elements.sent-2411-016: The Einstein's gravity with a negative cosmological constant allows us to consider a holographic picture where a black hole is a system that is dual to the conformal field theory (CFT) \cite{Maldacena,Witten,Witten1 .}
elements.sent-2411-017: The holography (AdS/CFT correspondence) can help us to solve nontrivial problems in condensed matter physics \cite{Kovtun,Kovtun1,Hartnoll .}
elements.sent-2411-018: The heat engine of a black hole as the working substance was studied firstly by Johnson \cite{Johnson .}
elements.sent-2411-019: It was shown that considering a closed path in the $P-V$ plane the useful mechanical work of static AdS black holes can be obtained from heat energy.
elements.sent-2411-020: For Born--Infeld electrodynamics in AdS space a black hole as a heat engine was studied in \cite{Johnson3 .}
elements.sent-2411-021: The authors of Ref. \cite{Mann1 studied the effects of spacetime dimension, rotation, and higher curvature corrections on the efficiency of the holographic heat engine cycle.}
elements.sent-2411-022: It is worth noting that the efficiency depends on the black hole (working substance) equation of state and the choice of the cycle.
elements.sent-2411-023: In \cite{Johnson4,Johnson7 a benchmarking scheme was described that allows to compare the efficiencies of black holes (as working substances) in heat engines for a circular cycle in the $P-V$ plane.}
elements.sent-2411-024: Studies of holographic heat engines employing different black hole heat engines were considered in Refs.
elements.sent-2411-025: This paper studies the heat engine of a magnetic black hole in Einstein-AdS gravity coupled to rational nonlinear electrodynamics (RNED) which was proposed in \cite{Kruglov (see also \cite{Kruglov1 ).}}
elements.sent-2411-026: It was proven by Bronnikov \cite{Bronnikov that electrically charged black holes with the Lagrangian function ${\cal L ({\cal F )$, possessing the Maxwell limit at weak fields, do not have a regular center for a static, spherically symmetric solution of general relativity.}}}
elements.sent-2411-027: Therefore, we will study only magnetically charged black holes.
elements.sent-2411-028: In the case electrically charged black holes, thermodynamic behavior of black holes will be different because of equation of state.
elements.sent-2411-029: It is of interest also to study how quantum corrections can influence the thermodynamic properties and efficiency of the black hole heat engine.
elements.sent-2411-030: We will study this in further.
elements.sent-2411-031: We consider also holographic heat engines of our model in the vicinity of a critical point.
elements.sent-2411-032: The black hole thermodynamics in extended phase space in the framework of Einstein-AdS-RNED was studied in \cite{Kruglov2 .}
elements.sent-2411-033: The Einstein–-Born–-Infeld solution, critical points and stability were investigated in \cite{Behnam .}
elements.sent-2411-034: In \cite{Behnam1 the effect of the non-perturbative correction on the thermodynamics quantities was studied.}
elements.sent-2411-035: The RNED is similar to Born--Infeld electrodynamics which is without singularities at the center and at weak-field limit it is converted into the Maxwell electrodynamics.
elements.sent-2411-036: But there are differences: the Born--Infeld Lagrangian at high field limit becomes infinite but for RNED one has $\mbox{lim _{{\cal F}\rightarrow\infty {\cal L ({\cal F )=-1/(8\pi\beta)$ which is finite.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-037: It was stated in \cite{Bronnikov2 that NED models with finite limit at high fields are preferable.}
elements.sent-2411-038: The comparison of RNED with other NED models was done in \cite{Bronnikov2 .}
elements.sent-2411-039: In addition, the Born--Infeld theory does not admit the birefringence phenomena which is predicted by QED that is now on the experimental verification.
elements.sent-2411-040: At the same time the Generalized Born--Infeld theory \cite{Kruglov5 (see also \cite{Kruglov6 ) as well as RNED (with the additional therm $a(E\cdotB)^2$ in RNED Lagrangian \cite{Kruglov7 ) describes the birefringence effect.}}}
elements.sent-2411-041: The gravity coupled to RNED describes the universe inflation \cite{Kruglov3 and reproduces the shadow of $M87^*$ black hole \cite{Kruglov4 .}}
elements.sent-2411-042: Here we use Einstein–AdS–RNED black hole as the working substance.
elements.sent-2411-043: We will show that within our model the heat engine of black holes can produce work.
elements.sent-2411-044: The structure of the paper is as follows.
elements.sent-2411-045: In Section 2 we review the model of Einstein-AdS gravity coupled to RNED.
elements.sent-2411-046: The thermodynamic variables such as the metric function, black hole mass, Hawking temperature and pressure are given.
elements.sent-2411-047: The black hole heat efficiency is studied in Section 3.
elements.sent-2411-048: A rectangle closed path in the $P-V$ plane and Carnot's cycle are considered.
elements.sent-2411-049: We have obtained and compared efficiencies for these two cycles at some model parameters.
elements.sent-2411-050: The holographic heat engine cycle in the vicinity of a critical point for our model is studied in Section 4.
elements.sent-2411-051: Section 5 is the summery of results obtained.
elements.sent-2411-052: We will employ geometrical units with $G=c=\hbar=k_B=1$.
elements.sent-2411-053: Einstein-AdS gravity coupled to RNED is given by the action \cite{Kruglov2 \begin{equation}I=\int d^{4}x\sqrt{-g}\left(\frac{R-2\Lambda}{16\pi}+\mathcal{L}(\mathcal{F}) \right), \label{2}\end{equation} where $\Lambda=-3/l^2$, $l$ is the AdS radius and the RNED Lagrangian is given by \begin{equation}{\cal L}=-\frac{{\cal F}}{4\pi(1+2\beta {\cal F})}. \label{3}\end{equation} ${\cal F =F^{\mu\nu F_{\mu\nu /4=(B^2-E^2)/2$, and $E$ and $B$ are the electric and magnetic fields.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-054: We will study magnetic black holes and, therefore, $E=0$, $B=q/r^2$ with $q$ being a magnetic charge.
elements.sent-2411-055: The spherical symmetry solution for the metric function is \begin{equation}f(r)=1-\frac{2m(r)}{r}-\frac{\Lambda r^2}{3}, \label{4}\end{equation} where the mass function reads \cite{Kruglov2 \begin{equation}m(r)=m_0+\frac{q^{3/2}g(r)}{8\sqrt{2}\beta^{1/4}},~~~~ g(r)\equiv \ln\left(\frac{r^2-\sqrt{2q}\beta^{1/4}r+q\sqrt{\beta}}{r^2+\sqrt{2q}\beta^{1/4}r+q\sqrt{\beta}}\right)\end{equation} \begin{equation}+2\arctan\left(\frac{\sqrt{q}\beta^{1/4}+\sqrt{2}r}{\sqrt{q}\beta^{1/4}}\right) -2\arctan\left(\frac{\sqrt{q}\beta^{1/4}-\sqrt{2}r}{\sqrt{q}\beta^{1/4}}\right) \label{5}\end{equation} and $m_0$ is the Schwarzschild mass (the integration constant).}
elements.sent-2411-056: The BH magnetic mass is given by \begin{equation}m_M=4\pi\int_0^\infty \rho_M r^2dr=\frac{\pi q^{3/2}}{4\sqrt{2}\beta^{1/4}},~~~~\rho_M =\frac{q^2}{8\pi (r^4+q^2\beta)}, \label{6}\end{equation} where $\rho_M$ is the density of the magnetic energy.
elements.sent-2411-057: From equation $f(r_+)=0$, where $r_+$ is the event horizon radius, and Eqs.
elements.sent-2411-058: From Eq. (8) we find Equation of State (EoS) \begin{equation}P=\frac{T}{2r_+}-\frac{1}{8\pi r_+^2}+\frac{q^2}{8\pi (r_+^4+\beta q^2)}. \label{9}\end{equation} One can verify from Eqs.
elements.sent-2411-059: The event horizon radius $r_+$, which is the higher root of equation $f(r_+)=0$, can be replaced by $S$ or $V $ because they are not independent.
elements.sent-2411-060: It is worth noting that at $\beta=0$ the Hawking temperature (8) and pressure (9) become $T$ and $P$ corresponding to Reissner–-Nordstr\"{o m-AdS space.}
elements.sent-2411-061: It should be noted that at great charge $q$ and small $\beta$ the Hawking temperature (8) becomes negative, i.e. nonphysical.
elements.sent-2411-062: A heat engine is defined as a closed path in the $P-V$ plane with $P(V,T)$ being EoS.
elements.sent-2411-063: The input amount of heat is $Q_H$ and the exhaust amount is $Q_C$ with the total mechanical work done $W=Q_H-Q_C$.
elements.sent-2411-064: Then the efficiency of the heat engine is given by \begin{equation}\eta=\frac{W}{Q_H}=1-\frac{Q_C}{Q_H} \label{10}\end{equation} and it depends upon a closed path and EoS.
elements.sent-2411-065: The heats $Q_H$ and $Q_C$ may be treated as going from heat baths of radiation around black holes \cite{Hawking .}
elements.sent-2411-066: In the classic maximally efficient Carnot cycle two isotherms are connected by two adiabatic paths while in the classic Stirling cycle two isochoric paths connect two temperatures.
elements.sent-2411-067: It was shown in \cite{Johnson that for static black holes, the Carnot engine is also a Stirling engine and they are reversible because the total entropy flow is zero.}
elements.sent-2411-068: For the static black holes the thermodynamic volume and the entropy are not independent and, therefore, that adiabats and isochores are the same for Carnot and Stirling cycles.
elements.sent-2411-069: The Carnot efficiency is given by the temperature difference, $\eta_C=W/Q_H=1-T_C/T_H$.
elements.sent-2411-070: Here, a pair of isotherms are at temperatures $T_H$ and $T_C$, where in isothermal expansion some heat is absorbed at temperatures $T_H$, and in isothermal compression some heat gets out at the temperature $T_C$.
elements.sent-2411-071: The heat flow is shown in Fig. 1.
elements.sent-2411-072: The upper and lower isobars give the net flow of heat $Q_H$ and $Q_C$, correspondingly.
elements.figure-2411-001: \includegraphics [height=2.7in,width=5in] {HeatEngine2.eps} \caption{\label{fig.1} A rectangle closed path in the $P - V$ plane and two isotherms with $T_C=0.7$, $T_H=1$, $\beta=1$, $q=1$, $r_+=\sqrt[3]{3V/(4\pi)}$.}
elements.sent-2411-073: \begin{equation}Q_H=\int_{T_1}^{T_2} C_P(P_1,T)dT,~~~~Q_C=\int_{T_1}^{T_2} C_P(P_2,T)dT. \label{11}\end{equation} It should be noted that there is not the heat exchange in the isochoric process because $C_V=0$ (processes corresponding to vertical arrows in Fig. 1).
elements.sent-2411-074: The heat capacity is given by \begin{equation}C_P=T\left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial T}\right)_{P,q}=\frac{T\partial S/\partial r_+}{\partial T/\partial r_+}=\frac{2\pi r_+ T}{\partial T/\partial r_+}, \label{12}\end{equation} where the black hole entropy $S=\pi r_+^2$.
elements.formula-2411-011: \begin{equation}C_P=T\left(\frac{\partial S}{\partial T}\right)_{P,q}=\frac{T\partial S/\partial r_+}{\partial T/\partial r_+}=\frac{2\pi r_+ T}{\partial T/\partial r_+}, \label{12}\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-076: It is worth mentioning that the efficiency of the heat engine (10) with $Q_H$ and $Q_C$ given in Eq. (13) can be written through the black hole mass (7) in accordance with the formula presented in \cite{Johnson1 .}
elements.sent-2411-077: The useful work done in our holographic engine is $W=Q_H-Q_C=0.5(r^3_2-r^3_1)(1/l_1^2-1/l_2^2)$.
elements.sent-2411-078: Thus to increase the engine useful work one can increase the difference $r_2-r_1$ or/and $\Lambda_2-\Lambda_1$ ($\Lambda=-3/l^2$) of cycles.
elements.figure-2411-002: \includegraphics [height=2.7in,width=5in] {HeatEngine1.eps} \caption{\label{fig.2} The black hole heat efficiencies for a cycle with two isobars and two isochores/adiabats (left panel) for $q=1$, $r_1=1$, $r_2=10$, $l_1=1$, $l_2=10$ and two isotherms and two isochores/adiabats (right panel for Carnot's cycle) for $q=1$, $l_1=1$, $l_2=10$, $r_1=1$, and $r_2=10$.}
elements.sent-2411-080: The $T_H$ corresponds to $P_1$ and the $T_C$ corresponds to $P_2$ within EoS, Eq. (9).
elements.sent-2411-081: Figure 2 shows that the black hole heat efficiency $\eta$ for a cycle with two isobars and two isochores/adiabats is less than $\eta_C$ for two isotherms and two isochores/adiabats for a Carnot cycle (for some range of coupling $\beta$).
elements.sent-2411-082: According to Fig. 2 when coupling $\beta$ increases the black hole heat efficiencies $\eta$ and $\eta_C$ decrease.
elements.sent-2411-083: The ratio $\eta/\eta_C$ is plotted in Fig. 3.
elements.figure-2411-003: \includegraphics [height=2.7in,width=5in] {HeatEngine4.eps} \caption{\label{fig.3} The ratio $\eta/\eta_C$ vs. $\beta$ for $q=1$, $r_1=1$, $r_2=10$, $l_1=1$, $l_2=10$. For $\beta>1$ we have nonphysical case as $\eta>\eta_C$.}
elements.sent-2411-084: Figure 3 shows that at some range of coupling $\beta$ we have $\eta<\eta_C$, but for grater $\beta$ one has $\eta>\eta_C$ (the nonphysical case).
elements.sent-2411-085: This can be explained by the fact that RNED becomes Maxwell electrodynamics at the limit $\beta\rightarrow 0$.
elements.sent-2411-086: Therefore, the physical case takes place only for small $\beta$.
elements.sent-2411-087: When $\beta\rightarrow 0$ we come to RN-AdS spacetime.
elements.sent-2411-088: The ratio $\eta/\eta_C$ monotonously increases with the increase of coupling $\beta$ and for large $\beta$ the relation $\eta>\eta_C$ holds that contradicts the traditional thermodynamics that the Carnot heat engine possesses the highest efficiency which is also nonphysical case.
elements.figure-2411-004: \includegraphics [height=2.7in,width=5in] {HeatEngine3.eps} \caption{\label{fig.4} Left panel: The black hole heat efficiencies for a cycle with two isobars and two isochores/adiabats for $\beta=1$, $r_1=1$, $r_2=10$, $l_1=1$, $l_2=10$. Right panel for Carnot's cycle: Heat efficiency of two isotherms and two isochores/adiabats for $\beta=1$, $r_1=1$, and $r_2=10$, $l_1=1$, $l_2=10$.}
elements.sent-2411-089: In accordance with Fig. 4 if magnetic charge $q$ increases the black hole heat efficiencies $\eta$ and $\eta_C$ also increase.
elements.sent-2411-090: For Carnot's cycle (right panel) the black hole heat efficiency is bounded, $\eta_C<1$ showing the conversion of heat into work.
elements.sent-2411-091: Left panel of Fig. 4 shows two regimes: 1) when $\eta \leq 1$ there is the conversion of heat into work (the physical case); 2) if $\eta > 1$ we have creating a temperature difference through the application of work to the system which is probably nonphysical case.
elements.sent-2411-092: One can speculate that this is a refrigeration system - the work is produced by surrounds.
elements.sent-2411-093: But for large charge $q$ and small $\beta$ the temperature becomes negative (see Eq. 8) and, therefore, the system is nonphysical (see \cite{Johnson2 for the case of RN-AdS spacetime with the electrical charge).}
elements.sent-2411-094: These two regimes are separated by equation $\eta=1$ ($Q_C=0$) \begin{equation}\frac{r_2-r_1}{2}+\frac{r_2^3-r_1^3}{2l_2^2}-\frac{q^{3/2}\left(g(r_2)-g(r_1)\right)}{8\sqrt{2}\beta^{1/4}}=0. \label{14}\end{equation} This equation depends on many parameters: $V_1$, $V_2$ (or $r_1$, $r_2$), $q$, $\beta$ and $l_2$ (or $P_2$) (see the rectangle cycle in Fig. 1).
elements.sent-2411-095: In figures 1-3 we have considered just an example for a particular choice of parameters of a cycle.
elements.sent-2411-096: The solution to Eq. (14) for parameters given in Fig. 3 is $q\approx 8.1857$.
elements.sent-2411-097: It follows from Eq. (10) that the efficiency of the heat engine $\eta>1$ when $Q_H$ or $Q_C$ is negative which corresponds to a refrigeration system.
elements.sent-2411-098: One can verify that for parameters $\beta=1$, $r_1=1$, and $r_2=10$, $l_1=1$, $l_2=10$ in Fig. 4, we have $Q_H\approx 474.34$ and $Q_C\approx -20.17$ for $q=20$ and $\eta=1.043$ that is the refrigeration system.
elements.sent-2411-099: It is worth noting that Johnson \cite{Johnson2 demonstrated that the black hole heat engine at the critical point approaches the Carnot cycle when the electric charge increases.}
elements.sent-2411-100: It was described there how to choose the physical cycle which touches the critical point.
elements.sent-2411-101: The same procedure can be used for our model by taking into account critical points found in \cite{Kruglov2 .}
elements.section-2411-004: Cycle in the vicinity of a critical point
elements.sent-2411-102: Firstly, the cycles of holographic heat engines close to critical points were considered in \cite{Johnson2 .}
elements.sent-2411-103: Johnson showed that an efficiency of holographic heat engine of a cycle in the vicinity of this critical point approaches the Carnot efficiency.
elements.sent-2411-104: Here, we will study holographic heat engines cycle in the vicinity of a critical point for our model.
elements.sent-2411-105: We demonstrate the limit $\eta\rightarrow \eta_C$ as expected due to general universality for a black hole holographic heat engine near critical points \cite{Mann2 .}
elements.sent-2411-106: The sub-leading term in an efficiency of a heat engine includes the van der Waals ratio $Tc/(v_cP_c)=8/3$ ($v_c$ is the critical specific volume in our model) plus the term with RNED coupling and the parameters $x<1$, $y<1$ ($\Delta V=xV_c$, $\Delta P=yP_c$) \cite{Mann2 .}
elements.sent-2411-107: It was shown in \cite{Mann2 that near critical point expansion of any models is given by \begin{equation}\rho=A\tau+B_0\tau\omega^{\delta-1/\beta}+C_0\omega^\delta+..., \label{15}\end{equation} where parameters $\rho$, $\tau$ and $\omega$ are connected with the pressure $P$, temperature $T$ and volume $V$ as $P=P_c(\rho+1)$, $T=T_c(\tau+1)$, $V=V_c(\omega+1)$.}
elements.sent-2411-108: Taking into account the values of critical exponents in our model \cite{Kruglov2 $\delta=3$, $\beta=1/2$ (do not mix this $\beta$ with the RNED coupling $\beta$), we obtain (in our notations) \begin{equation}p=\rho+1=1+At-Bt\omega+C\omega^3+..., \label{16}\end{equation} where (see \cite{Kruglov2 ) \begin{equation}A=\frac{1}{\rho_c}=\frac{8}{3}-\frac{2}{27q^2}\beta+{\cal O}(\beta^2),~~\rho_c=\frac{3}{8}+\frac{\beta}{96q^2}+ {\cal O}(\beta^2),~~B=\frac{1}{3\rho_c}=\frac{1}{3}A. \label{17}\end{equation} Then, making use of the general expression for the efficiency of the cycle $\eta$ in the $x\rightarrow 0$ limit \cite{Mann2 , we obtain \begin{equation}\eta=\frac{y}{A+y}-\frac{yB_0}{(A+y)^2(\delta-1/\beta+1)}(-x)^{\delta-1/\beta}+{\cal O}(x^{2\delta-2/\beta})\end{equation} \begin{equation}=\frac{y}{A+y}-\frac{xyA}{6(A+y)^2}+{\cal O}(x^2). \label{18}\end{equation} Note that the heat engine efficiency (18) depends only on the parameter $A$ which includes RNED coupling $\beta$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-109: The Carnot efficiency $\eta_C =1-T_C/T_H$ is given by \cite{Mann2 \begin{equation}\eta_C =\frac{y}{A+y}+\frac{C_0}{A+y}(-x)^\delta+...=\frac{y}{A+y}+{\cal O}(x^3), \label{19}\end{equation} where we have used the value $\delta=3$.}
elements.sent-2411-110: Equations (18) and (19) show that $\eta/\eta_C<1$ as it was expected.
elements.sent-2411-111: Equation (20) shows that heat efficiency $\eta$ approaches to the Carnot efficiency and is defined mostly by parameter $x$ because $xy\ll x$.
elements.sent-2411-112: The expression (20) is close to Johnson’s formula for the ratio of efficiency $\eta$ to the Carnot efficiency $\eta_C$ in the vicinity of critical points of charged AdS black holes.
elements.sent-2411-113: We have studied the heat engine of magnetically charged black holes as the working substance within Einstein's gravity in AdS space coupled to rational nonlinear electrodynamics.
elements.sent-2411-114: The dynamical negative cosmological constant in extended phase space is treated as a thermodynamic pressure and black hole mass as an enthalpy.
elements.sent-2411-115: The efficiencies of black hole heat engines for rectangle closed path in the $P-V$ plane and for Carnot cycle were obtained.
elements.sent-2411-116: We have demonstrated that the black hole efficiencies for both cycles decrease when the nonlinear electrodynamics coupling $\beta$ increases and the black hole efficiencies increase if the magnetic charge increases.
elements.sent-2411-117: For small coupling $\beta$ we have the physical case $\eta<\eta_C$ but for grater $\beta$ one has nonphysical case $\eta>\eta_C$.
elements.sent-2411-118: For the Carnot cycle the black hole efficiency $\eta_C$ is bounded, $\eta_C<1$, but for rectangle closed path the efficiency grows with magnetic charge $q$ showing that when $\eta<1$ we have work done by black holes and if $\eta\geq 1$ one has a refrigeration system - the work is produced by surrounds (the nonphysical case as the temperature can be negative).
elements.sent-2411-119: An efficiency of the holographic heat engine of a cycle in the vicinity of a critical point was studied.
elements.sent-2411-120: Our result is similar to Johnson's result \cite{Johnson2 for electrically charged AdS black holes.}
elements.sent-2411-121: One can employ the benchmarking scheme of \cite{Johnson4 to compare the heat efficiency of our model with heat efficiencies of other models.}
elements.sent-2411-122: It should be noted that the stability analysis of black holes is important for models to be viable.
elements.sent-2411-123: We leave such study for further.
elements.sent-2411-124: Our model of black hole physics in AdS space can be of interest for the study of gauge theories through holography.
elements.sent-2411-125: Data Availability Statement: No Data associated in the manuscript.
elements.sent-2411-126: Competing interests: The authors declare there are no competing interests.
